Record Class OutboxEvent

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.OutboxEvent
Record Components:
id - optional event identifier. When null, the implementation generates one (UUIDv7 in outbox-jdbc).
aggregateType - non-blank; per-dialect width limits apply at publish time.
aggregateId - non-blank; per-dialect width limits apply at publish time.
eventType - non-blank; per-dialect width limits apply at publish time.
contentType - non-blank (e.g. application/json); per-dialect width limits apply at publish time.
payload - opaque bytes; the library never deserializes it.
headers - string-to-string metadata; never null, may be empty.
destination - optional routing hint (topic / exchange / queue) as a transport-agnostic logical name, e.g. "orders" — never a URI like "kafka://orders". The broker family is conveyed out-of-band (relay configuration, headers, or the messaging.system span attribute), not embedded here, because the publisher does not know which transport the relay will use. Per-dialect width limits apply at publish time when present.
occurredAt - the domain timestamp; never null.
public record OutboxEvent(UUID id, String aggregateType, String aggregateId, String eventType, String contentType, byte[] payload, Map<String,String> headers, String destination, Instant occurredAt) extends Record
One row to be written to the outbox table.

Construct instances through builder(). The compact constructor enforces field invariants and makes a defensive copy of headers and payload; the corresponding accessors return immutable / fresh copies to keep the record effectively immutable.

Field semantics match the columns documented in AGENTS.md > Table contract.

Length / width validation is intentionally not enforced here. The record carries no opinion on the SQL column widths because (a) adopters may legitimately run a customized schema with wider columns, and (b) different databases measure column width in characters or in bytes, which is a dialect concern. The dialect SPI (see OutboxDialect#validate(OutboxEvent) in outbox-jdbc) owns those checks and runs them at publish time so callers still get a fail-fast error before any SQL is sent.

Field validation failures (null required component, blank string, null header key or value) surface as OutboxValidationException — a leaf of the sealed OutboxException hierarchy. Since 0.4.0 the builder and the compact constructor no longer raise raw NullPointerException / IllegalArgumentException; a single catch (OutboxException e) around builder()...build() plus publish(event) is enough.

Equality: the record uses the default component-wise equality. Because payload is a byte[], two events with identical bytes but distinct array instances are not equal. This is intentional — within a process the id discriminates events, and outside of tests payload-equality is not a useful operation.

Since:
0.1.0

    OutboxEvent(UUID id, String aggregateType, String aggregateId, String eventType, String contentType, byte[] payload, Map<String,String> headers, String destination, Instant occurredAt)
    Canonical compact constructor: validates every component (non-null, non-blank) and makes a defensive copy of payload and headers so the resulting record is effectively immutable.

  • Constructor Details

    • OutboxEvent

      public OutboxEvent(UUID id, String aggregateType, String aggregateId, String eventType, String contentType, byte[] payload, Map<String,String> headers, String destination, Instant occurredAt)
      Canonical compact constructor: validates every component (non-null, non-blank) and makes a defensive copy of payload and headers so the resulting record is effectively immutable. Per-dialect width limits are not enforced here — see OutboxDialect#validate(OutboxEvent) in outbox-jdbc.
      Throws:
      OutboxValidationException - if a required component is null, a string component is blank, or headers contains a null key or value.

  • Method Details

    • payload

      public byte[] payload()
      Returns a defensive copy of payload.

      Callers that only need the size in bytes should prefer payloadSize(), which avoids cloning the underlying array.

      Returns:
      a fresh copy of the payload bytes.

    • payloadSize

      public int payloadSize()
      Returns the size in bytes of the payload, without cloning the underlying array. Intended for callers (metrics, logging) that need the size and never the bytes.
      Returns:
      the payload length in bytes.
      Since:
      0.1.0

    • headers

      public Map<String,String> headers()
      Returns the headers map. Already immutable; safe to return as-is.
      Returns:
      the immutable headers map.

    • builder

      public static OutboxEvent.Builder builder()
      Creates a fresh OutboxEvent.Builder.
      Returns:
      a new, mutable OutboxEvent.Builder initialized with no fields set.
      Since:
      0.1.0

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.

      public UUID id()
      Returns the value of the id record component.
      Returns:
      the value of the id record component
